summary.intent: Address a Codex automated review finding on PR #668 in the ars-ui repo by fixing a bug where the disabled guard on ToggleButton was blocking prop-sync SetPressed events in addition to user activation events.
summary.outcome: The fix was committed (follow-up to commit 25c3201d) on the existing PR #668 branch: disabled state now only blocks user-initiated activation events while allowing SetPressed for controlled prop synchronization, with regression tests added and spec text updated.

repo: The ars-ui repo uses cargo-mutants for mutation testing as a required gate before committing fixes; the default run tests ~82 mutants for the toggle_button module.

PR workflow includes: push branch, open PR, add 'snapshot-reviewed' label for PRs with .snap files, then trigger '@codex review' bot comment and poll for reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved) and unresolved threads.
Codex review bot signals completion by dropping the 👀 reaction; unresolved threads from codex user indicate findings that must be addressed.
The project runs cargo llvm-cov for coverage and cargo xtask spec validate for spec conformance checks.
CI checks include: Formatting, Snapshot Change Check, Workspace Check, and Clippy.

code.finding: The disabled guard was incorrectly blocking SetPressed events used for controlled prop synchronization, not just user activation events. Fix: narrow the guard to only block user-initiated Press/Release/Toggle events while allowing SetPressed through regardless of disabled state.
Spec text needed updating to clarify that 'disabled' blocks user activation events only, not programmatic SetPressed prop sync.

workflow: Codex review polling used a 120-second sleep loop checking gh API for reactions and unresolved GraphQL review threads; typical review took 6-8 polling cycles (~12-16 minutes).
Mutation testing (cargo mutants) runs significantly longer when baseline test timing raises the per-mutant timeout; 82 mutants took well over 30 minutes for this module.
Artifact files (mutants.out/caught.txt, missed.txt, debug.log) can be tailed to monitor mutation progress without interrupting the run.
The review-fix commit flow: write regression tests that fail on old code → apply fix → confirm tests pass → run coverage → run mutation testing → clippy → commit.

friction: Mutation testing ran unusually long (estimated 30-45+ minutes) due to elevated per-mutant timeout from baseline timing, causing significant session wait time with repeated polling.
Codex review took 6-8 two-minute polling cycles before completing, requiring sustained session attention.
CI checks (Workspace Check, Clippy) were still pending after multiple polling rounds with no clear ETA visible from the transcript.
open_items: CI status for Workspace Check and Clippy was still pending when the session ended; need to verify these pass on the pushed review-fix commit.
The Codex review thread on PR #668 needs a reply confirming the fix was applied so the thread can be resolved.
A new '@codex review' trigger may be needed after the fix commit to get final Codex approval on PR #668.
